title: Drew Baird
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Drew Baird
Snapshot: 2026-07-21

confidence.rationale: Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.
Proposed from an explicit normalized Knowledge Object field.

biography: A native of Celebration, Florida- Dr. Baird is an avid hiker, cook, concert goer, and occasional scuba diver. His teaching specializations include the sociology of education, health and illness, and statistics for the social sciences.
education_entries: Ph D in Sociology, University of Central Florida
MA in Sociology, University of West Georgia
BS in Sociology, Florida State University
research_interests: Higher education; For-profit education; Social mobility; Substance use; Addiction; Homelessness; Racial discourse; Racial attitudes
teaching_interests: Sociology of Education; Sociology of Health; Quantitative Methodology
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal (2026). An Inseverable Link to the Outside: Describing Shifting Food Consumption Patterns and Predicting Food Dysregulation During COVID Lockdown . Journal of Professional and Public Sociology.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2023). Understanding the Rise of Anti-Political Correctness Sentiment: The Curious Role of Education . Humanity and Society. Volume, 47. Issue, 1.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2019). Seeking Evidence of Grade Inflation at For-Profit Universities . Sociological Focus. Volume, 52. Issue, 4. Pages, 343-358.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2019). Veiled Threats: Color-Blind Frames and Group Threat in Affirmative Action Discourse . Social Problems.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2019). Selling the Middle Class For-Profit: Examining the Prospect of ‘Good Jobs’ for For-Profit College Graduate . The Journal of Public and Professional Sociology. Volume, 11. Issue, 1.
Journal Article, Academic Journal (2014). “How Can We Stay Sober?” Homeless Women’s Experience in a Substance Abuse Treatment Center . Society. Volume, 51.
